summaries: "Világok harca: Mars-lakók a földön" by H. G. Wells is a science fiction novel published in 1898. When mysterious cylinders crash near London, Martian invaders emerge with devastating heat-rays and towering war machines. A nameless narrator struggles to survive and reunite with his wife as the aliens systematically destroy southern England. All organized resistance crumbles before the superior technology of these extraterrestrial attackers. This groundbreaking story of humanity's conflict with beings from another world explores themes of evolution, imperialism, and Victorian-era fears while establishing the alien invasion genre.
